behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: collector outputs from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log must all be present in the payload sent to the summary generator. A unit test of one function can miss wiring/aggregation omissions between modules (MOCK-MISS=yes). Real OS/network is not required for this specific behavior; in-process fakes for the three source clients reproduce omission/merge bugs (REAL-INFRA=no, so not System). Correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on presence of fields in the handoff payload (LLM-DEP=no, so not Agentic). No multi-agentic sequence (MULTI-STEP=no, so not Workflow). Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text 'content passed ... includes data retrieved from all three sources' because this is a data-plumbing assertion, not real-infra or model-quality validation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector/orchestrator modules with three stub source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; invoke one report build and assert the exact request payload passed to the summary-generation interface contains all three source sections and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback formatting: source adapter failure must be converted into section-level placeholder text while pipeline continues (MOCK-MISS=yes). In-process failure injection (adapter raises timeout/5xx exception) is sufficient to reproduce the real failure mode for this behavior (REAL-INFRA=no for minimum). Mocking does not inherently hide the bug here because fallback logic is local orchestration, not transport stack behavior. No dependence on model output quality (LLM-DEP=no) and no multi-agentic chain (MULTI-STEP=no). Integration-brake checks: (a) real OS/network/subprocess not required for stated fallback behavior, (b) no AI-output correctness criterion, (c) no multi-step agentic sequence, (d) no LLM-governed stage handoff requirement in behavior text. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic disqualified by behavior text focusing on 'data unavailable note ... rather than aborting' (control-flow fallback), not infra realism or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with real orchestration modules, two source stubs returning normal data, and one stub throwing an availability error; assert pipeline completes, report includes `data unavailable` in only the failing section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality (faithfulness to key values), which is model-output dependent; deterministic stubs would mask hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors (LLM-DEP=yes). Unit/Integration can verify prompt wiring but cannot validate real model judgment. System alone is insufficient because even with real HTTP transport, fake model output would pass. MULTI-STEP is not required by behavior text; it asks about one summary outcome, not multiple sequential model decisions, so Workflow is not minimum. Rejection-gate: Workflow disqualified by singular behavior text 'The narrative summary accurately represents...' with no explicit chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on a fixed dataset with known key metrics/issues/deploy facts across repeated runs; score outputs against required fact-presence/consistency checks (e.g., key-value extraction match, no contradictory values) and fail if factual coverage/accuracy drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: The stated failure boundary includes real delivery to an inbox, which requires external email transport/network behavior and end-to-end process integration (REAL-INFRA=yes). In-process mocks can falsely pass while SMTP/API auth, TLS, provider acceptance, or inbox routing fails (MOCK-HIDE=yes), so System is minimum. Agentic is not required for this specific assertion because success criterion is delivery to recipient inbox, not model-output quality; summary text can be fixed for this test without disqualifying the behavior. No explicit multi-agentic sequence requirement (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text 'reaches ... inbox correctly' which targets transport E2E, not LLM decision quality or chained agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end against a test environment with reachable source endpoints and a real email test inbox/service; verify one run produces an email received by configured recipient with expected report structure and attached/embedded content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is orchestration/state persistence across runs: on send failure, write fallback artifact and retry later. This can be reproduced with real local filesystem plus a failing mail client stub and two invocations (MOCK-MISS=yes, Unit too low). Minimum does not require real network outage or OS scheduler: deterministic send-failure injection and simulated next run catch the core bug (REAL-INFRA not strictly required for minimum; local filesystem fits Integration scope). LLM-output quality is irrelevant (LLM-DEP=no). No multiple agentic steps (MULTI-STEP=no). Integration-brake checks: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess boundary to reproduce stated logic, (b) no AI/ML correctness dependency, (c) no chained agentic stages, (d) no LLM-governed handoff in behavior text. Rejection-gate: System disqualified by behavior text emphasizing 'saved to a local file' and 'retried on the next scheduled run'—stateful retry logic can fail independent of real email infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Use real report-generation/orchestration modules with mail sender stubbed to fail first run then succeed second run; execute run #1 and assert fallback file is written with report content, then execute run #2 and assert queued file is retried/sent and retry state is cleared.
